title: erigeron glabratus subsp. glabratus
intro: Erigeron glabratus subsp. glabratus, also known as the downy fleabane, is a flowering plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae). This perennial herb has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The plant's health benefits include relief from pain, reduction of fever, and treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and nausea, due to its ability to ease spasms in the digestive tract. Erigeron glabratus subsp. glabratus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a popular herbal remedy in folk medicine.
